skill.name: github-automation
skill.description: Automate GitHub repository, issue, pull request, branch, workflow, deployment, and permission tasks through Rube MCP and GitHub OAuth.
skill.summary: Coordinate GitHub issue, pull request, repository, CI/CD, and permission workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives caused by Markdown code spans around Rube and GitHub tool names. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are also false positives from ordinary words and CI/CD text. Real risk remains medium because the skill guides OAuth-backed GitHub operations that can merge pull requests, delete repositories, trigger workflows, and change permissions.

medium_findings.title: OAuth-Backed GitHub State Changes
medium_findings.description: The skill requires an active GitHub connection through Rube MCP and documents tool sequences that create issues, create repositories, merge pull requests, trigger workflows, and update protection settings. These actions are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they can modify production repositories if the connected account has broad permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs use of GitHub OAuth via Rube MCP and lists repository-changing GitHub tools. The intent appears legitimate, but the impact depends on the connected account permissions.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Operations Require Strong User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill includes safety guidance to require confirmation before destructive operations, but it also references irreversible repository deletion and merging as supported workflows. Marketplace users should treat merge, delete, workflow dispatch, collaborator, and branch protection actions as privileged operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation names irreversible or hard-to-reverse GitHub actions and includes its own confirmation guidance. This supports a medium-risk operational finding rather than a malicious finding.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated external command alerts point to backticked Markdown identifiers for Rube and GitHub MCP tools. I found no evidence of Ruby code, shell command execution, eval behavior, or local process spawning in the only scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are Markdown code spans around tool slugs and parameter names. No executable source file or shell syntax is present in the scanned content.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines contain the YAML description, issue body field, safety text, and known-pitfall text, not calls to DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows ordinary documentation text, not cryptographic functions or configuration. The high static severity should be dismissed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded MCP URL Is Expected Setup Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Rube MCP endpoint that users must configure. This is expected for a Rube MCP GitHub automation skill and is not evidence of hidden exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in the setup section and matches the declared dependency on Rube MCP. No covert network destination or credential forwarding was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository Merge, Delete, Workflow, and Permission Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents tools for merging pull requests, creating repositories, triggering GitHub Actions workflows, updating branch protection, and adding collaborators. These patterns are powerful and should run only after explicit user authorization in the connected GitHub account.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool names and safety notes directly identify privileged GitHub actions. The pattern is dangerous by capability, although it is aligned with the skill purpose.

content.user_title: Automate GitHub Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: GitHub maintenance often requires many repeated repository, issue, pull request, and workflow steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Rube MCP workflows for those GitHub tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides connection setup for Rube MCP and GitHub OAuth.
Lists tool sequences for creating, searching, and commenting on GitHub issues.
Supports pull request discovery, creation, file review, CI checks, and merge workflows.
Covers repository creation, branch listing, branch creation, and repository setting updates.
Documents code search, commit search, workflow dispatch, deployments, collaborators, and branch protection checks.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active GitHub OAuth connection.
Cannot exceed the permissions granted to the connected GitHub account.
Does not implement its own GitHub client or validation layer.
GitHub search, pagination, and workflow limits still apply.

use_cases.title: Triage Repository Issues
use_cases.description: Find open issues, separate pull requests from issues, create new issues, and add follow-up comments.

use_cases.title: Review and Merge Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Inspect pull request details, list changed files, verify CI status, and prepare a merge after approval.

use_cases.title: Operate GitHub Automation Workflows
use_cases.description: Trigger GitHub Actions, inspect deployments, manage branches, and review repository permissions.

prompt_templates.title: List My Repositories
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub automation skill to confirm my Rube MCP GitHub connection, then list my repositories sorted by recent activity.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub automation skill to create an issue in OWNER/REPO with this title and body. Check for similar open issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub automation skill to inspect pull request NUMBER in OWNER/REPO, list changed files, check CI status, and summarize merge blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub automation skill to inspect branch protection, recent commits, open pull requests, check runs, and deployment status for OWNER/REPO before release.

output_examples.input: Check whether pull request 42 in acme/api is ready to merge.
output: Connection status confirmed through Rube MCP.
Pull request details reviewed, changed files listed, and CI checks inspected.
Result: not ready. One required check is failing and branch protection blocks merge.

output_examples.input: Create a bug report issue in acme/web for a broken login redirect.
output: Existing open issues searched for similar login redirect reports.
No duplicate was found in the selected repository.
A new issue was prepared with title, description, labels, and assignee fields.

output_examples.input: Review deployment status for the staging environment.
output: Repository workflows and deployments were queried through GitHub tools.
The latest staging deployment was found with its current status.
Next actions were summarized without changing repository state.

best_practices: Confirm the target owner, repository, branch, and pull request before any state-changing action.
Review CI status and branch protection before approving a merge or release step.
Use least-privilege GitHub OAuth permissions for the connected Rube MCP account.
anti_patterns: Do not merge, delete, dispatch workflows, or change permissions without explicit user approval.
Do not assume issue lists contain only issues, because GitHub issue endpoints can include pull requests.
Do not rely on a single page of results when listing repositories, issues, commits, or collaborators.

faq.question: Does this skill require a GitHub token?
faq.answer: It uses Rube MCP with a GitHub OAuth connection. Users must complete the connection before workflows can run.

faq.question: Can it merge pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents merge workflows, but merge actions should require explicit approval and CI review.

faq.question: Can it delete repositories?
faq.answer: The documentation mentions repository deletion as irreversible. Treat deletion as a privileged action that needs direct confirmation.

faq.question: Does it run local shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The backticked names in the file are Markdown tool identifiers, not executable shell commands.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the main limits?
faq.answer: It depends on Rube MCP availability, GitHub OAuth permissions, GitHub API pagination, and GitHub search limits.
